toolchain.swift_version:
Apple Swift version 6.2.3 (swiftlang-6.2.3.3.21 clang-1700.6.3.2)
Target: arm64-apple-macosx26.0

bullets.claim: Context compaction runs on every ChatService.streamCompletion turn before provider.buildRequest; currently truncate-only.
Token estimation for compaction is heuristic: ~1 token per 4 chars + 4 tokens/message overhead.
Stop button exists and calls ChatViewModel.stopGeneration(), but stopGeneration does not cancel the provider stream task; it only resets UI state and cancels tool executions.
Tool manifest injection into the system prompt is implemented centrally in ChatService before compaction.

top_risks.risk: No rolling-summary compaction path; summarizeOldest is a placeholder and never selected.
AnthropicProvider drops .system messages entirely (tool manifest and memory XML can be omitted from Anthropic requests).
Stop/reset is UI-only; provider network streams continue unless they naturally finish.

call_graph.notes: UI stop button wired to onStop.
Inject memories + tool manifest, then compact.
Uses compacted messages and optional tool schemas.

notes.claim: Default context compaction budget is 120,000 tokens with preserveRecentMessages=10 and preserveSystemPrompt=true.

evidence.excerpt:
/// Default configuration with sensible defaults.
    static let `default` = ContextConfig(
        enabled: true,
        defaultMaxTokens: 120_000, // Conservative default for most models
        preserveSystemPrompt: true,
        preserveRecentMessages: 10,
        providerOverrides: [:]
    )

notes.claim: Compaction is disabled by config.enabled; when disabled it returns original messages and uses compactor.estimateTokens(messages:) for stats.
Safe indices include (optional) first .system message and the last preserveRecentMessages messages.
Emergency compaction keeps only system (if configured) and newest message if still over budget.

limits_and_payloads.system_prompt_handling: System messages are dropped in AnthropicProvider mapping.

limits_and_payloads.notes: OpenAIProvider selects /responses vs /chat/completions; build path shown does not set max_tokens; responses builder shown has no max token parameter.

evidence.excerpt:
public func chatCompletion(
        messages: [ORMessage],
        model: String,
        temperature: Double? = nil,
        maxTokens: Int? = nil,
        stream: Bool = false,
        transforms: [String]? = nil,
        route: String? = nil
    ) async throws -> ORChatResponse {
        let payload = ORChatRequest(
            model: model,
            messages: messages,
            temperature: temperature,
            maxTokens: maxTokens,
            stream: stream,
            transforms: transforms,
            route: route
        )
